data.summary: Acme Battery Solutions Pvt. Ltd. is a Tamil Nadu-based EV battery vendor claiming over a decade of manufacturing experience in lithium-ion cells, battery management systems, and EV charging solutions. However, cross-referencing against the Udyam MSME registration database reveals a critical discrepancy: the company's incorporation date is late 2024, making the '12+ years of experience' claim on their website factually impossible. This is classified as a Critical Red Flag and a potential deal-breaker for any PO issuance.
techSignals: BMS firmware: proprietary claims, no GitHub or patent evidence
ISO 26262 functional safety certification claimed — not found in public registry
Website built on WordPress — no engineering blog or technical disclosure
No verifiable tech stack signals beyond basic website infrastructure

slgSignals: Contact form only — no self-serve pricing
WhatsApp Business number as primary sales channel
Bulk order inquiries via email only
pricingTiers: Custom quotes only — no published pricing
data.notes: Pure SLG motion with no digital self-serve. Pricing opacity is a yellow flag for vendor transparency.

certifications: ISO 26262 — CLAIMED on website, NOT found in public certification databases
IATF 16949 — claimed, no certificate number provided
techDebtSignals: No engineering blog, whitepaper, or technical disclosure
No patent filings in Controller General of Patents database
No product teardown reviews available online
data.manufacturingClaims: 200 MWh annual capacity claimed — no audit report available
data.notes: CRITICAL: All technical certifications claimed on website but none independently verifiable. This is consistent with the Udyam registration mismatch — vendor may be misrepresenting capabilities.

supplierRisks: Cell sourcing from China not disclosed — potential conflict minerals exposure
No supplier code of conduct published
data.notes: No ESG reporting whatsoever. For a vendor entering the EV supply chain of an OEM with EU export obligations, this is a significant gap — EU Battery Regulation 2023 will require lifecycle carbon data by 2025.
Not applicable. Hardware manufacturer with no AI product exposure.

directCompetitors.name: Exide Industries
directCompetitors.description: India's largest battery manufacturer. 75+ years of documented operations.

directCompetitors.name: Amara Raja Energy
directCompetitors.description: Established VRLA and Li-ion battery manufacturer with OEM supply agreements.

directCompetitors.name: Tata Green Batteries
directCompetitors.description: Tata Group-backed EV battery venture. Strong OEM relationships.

differentiators: Lower pricing than established players — reason unclear
Tamil Nadu proximity to Chennai OEM cluster
data.notes: No verifiable competitive differentiation. Pricing advantage is unexplained and potentially unsustainable.

dealBreakers: 🚨 CRITICAL — UDYAM MISMATCH: Website claims '12+ years of experience in battery manufacturing' (implies founded ~2012). Udyam Registration Number UDM-TN-33-XXXX-2024 shows incorporation in October 2024. This is a material misrepresentation of company history.
🚨 HIGH — CERTIFICATION FRAUD RISK: ISO 26262, AIS-156, and IATF 16949 certificates claimed on website. None traceable to an accredited certification body. Certificate numbers not provided.
warnings: No verifiable customer references (OEM supply agreements claimed but not documented)
Domain registered October 2024 — same month as Udyam registration
WhatsApp-only sales channel unusual for claimed manufacturing scale
lawsuits: None found in MCA records — company too new to have litigation history
regulatoryActions: No GST filings found matching claimed revenue scale — possible GST compliance gap
data.notes: DO NOT ISSUE PO. Two independent critical red flags: Udyam incorporation mismatch and unverifiable safety certifications. Recommend escalating to vendor fraud screening before any further engagement.
No verifiable financial data. Claimed revenue of ₹50Cr+ is inconsistent with company age (registered October 2024). MCA filings show paid-up capital of ₹10L only.

keyExecutives.name: Rajesh Kumar (claimed)
keyExecutives.title: Managing Director
keyExecutives.background: No LinkedIn profile found. No verifiable prior employment history. MCA filing shows director since 2024.

data.teamSize: Claimed 200+ employees — MCA filings show 12 employees at incorporation
data.notes: CRITICAL: Claimed executive backgrounds are not independently verifiable. Employee count discrepancy between website claims (200+) and MCA incorporation filing (12) is significant.

data.tam: ₹85,000 Cr India EV battery market by 2030
data.sam: ₹12,000 Cr Tier-2/3 EV battery component supply
marketTrends: PLI scheme incentivising domestic battery manufacturing
FAME-III subsidy driving OEM sourcing shift to Indian vendors
EU Battery Regulation forcing supply chain traceability from 2025
Hyundai, Tata, Mahindra accelerating vendor qualification programs
tailwinds: Government push for 30% EV penetration by 2030
Chennai automotive cluster is the supply chain epicentre
New entrant window before large players consolidate supply
headwinds: Exide and Amara Raja have entrenched OEM relationships
Chinese cell imports under anti-dumping scrutiny
Safety and certification bar rising due to EV fire incidents
data.notes: The market opportunity is real and large. However, this specific vendor's ability to participate is severely in question given the red flags identified.

data.documentTitle: Acme Battery Solutions FY2024 Annual Report
keyRisks: Financial data entirely unverifiable — no audited statements found
MCA filings show 10 employees vs. claimed 200+ workforce
No public annual report — /fetch returned no PDF document
Udyam registration mismatch flagged as potentially fraudulent
keyOpportunities: EV battery market growing at 28% CAGR in India
Government PLI scheme eligible if registration verified
highlights: No annual report PDF found via Linkup /fetch
Financial claims could not be verified from public filings
Fell back to Linkup /research investigate mode
MCA records contradicted revenue claims significantly

data.investmentThesis: Acme Battery Solutions presents an unacceptable vendor risk profile. Two critical, independently verifiable red flags — a Udyam registration mismatch proving fabricated company history, and unverifiable safety certifications — disqualify this vendor from PO issuance under any standard procurement compliance framework. The India EV battery market opportunity is genuine; this specific vendor is not.
keyStrengths: Geographic proximity to Chennai OEM cluster
Claimed pricing advantage vs. established players
Tamil Nadu state government EV incentive eligibility (if legitimate)
keyRisks: 🚨 CRITICAL: Website claims 12+ years experience; Udyam shows October 2024 incorporation
🚨 HIGH: Three safety certifications claimed; none traceable to accredited certification body
MCA filings show 12 employees vs. claimed 200+ workforce
No verifiable OEM supply agreements despite claims
Financial data entirely unverifiable — ₹50Cr revenue claim vs. ₹10L paid-up capital
data.recommendation: Do not issue PO. Escalate to vendor fraud screening immediately. Cross-reference director details (Rajesh Kumar) against MCA director blacklist and CIBIL commercial credit bureau. If engagement must continue, require physical factory audit with independent certification body present before any procurement decision.
